title: Caching Best Practices Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of caching best practices and their implementation logic.

questions.question: Which of these is the most common and robust caching pattern for modern web applications?
options: Read-Through
Write-Through
Cache-Aside (Lazy Loading)
Write-Behind

questions.question: What's the primary benefit of the 'Cache-Aside' strategy?
options: It's more secure than other strategies.
It's resilient to cache failures; the application can always fall back to the primary database.
It automatically generates a thumbail for each image.
It's the only way to store data in a database.

questions.question: Why is it important to set a TTL for EVERY cache key you create?
options: To make the database faster.
To ensure that stale data will eventually be deleted by Redis, even if your application's proactive invalidation fails.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To make the application more secure.

questions.question: What should the application do if the Redis server is temporarily unavailable?
options: Crash the original request with an error.
Wait until Redis is back online.
Gracefully fall back to the primary database and fulfill the request.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is the 'Cache Hit Ratio' and what does a high ratio (e.g., 90%) indicate?
options: It measures how fast the database is.
The percentage of requests served directly from the cache; a high ratio means your caching strategy is highly effective and significantly reduces database load.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.
